final_message:
Both files written and mechanically validated. All figures computed from `data/sales.csv` (revenue = `units * unit_price` per row, lines 2–31).

**`REPORT.md`** — 143 words (57% of the 250-word cap), exactly the four required H2 headings in order, no other headings, no forbidden words, exactly three bullets (17/13/15 words each, all ≤25).

**`summary.json`** — exactly the five required keys:
- `total_revenue`: 7154.44 (sum of 30 per-order revenues, exact in `Decimal`)
- `orders`: 30
- `median_order_value`: 204.94 — **edge case:** 30 orders is an even count, so the median is the mean of the 15th and 16th sorted values (190.00 from order 1003, `data/sales.csv:4`, and 219.89 from order 1020, `data/sales.csv:21`) = 204.945 exactly. I rounded to 204.94, which is what both Python's `round()` and `Decimal` half-even produce; only round-half-up would yield 204.95.
- `top_product_by_revenue`: "Gadget" at $2,392.00 — a 12.9% lead over the runner-up Widget ($2,118.94)
- `revenue_by_region`: North 2176.35, South 1732.48, East 1830.79, West 1414.82 — the four regional totals sum to exactly 7154.44, matching `total_revenue` with zero discrepancy

Compound pattern worth noting: Sprocket moves the most units (238, 44.9% of all 530 units sold across `data/sales.csv:2-31`) yet ranks last in revenue ($1,130.50, 15.8%) because of its $4.75 price — 3.2% of Gadget's $149.50. That volume-without-revenue gap is why the third recommendation targets Sprocket pricing while the first prioritizes Gadget. No commits made.
